query: As we increased our power levels, was there any counter-reaction?
response: Affirmative. The opposing force grew in direct proportion to the power output of the Enterprise.

query: So it kept us from forming a subspace field for the warp drive?
Computer, who is this L. Brahms?
response: Doctor Leah Brahms, engineer. Graduate of the Daystrom Institute Theoretical Propulsion Group. Galaxy class starships, team seven, junior member.

query: Call up subspace design logs.
response: Select menu. Visual records or L. Brahms' voice entries.

query: Specify programme.
Computer, is there a cross section image we can replicate on a holodeck?
response: Select menu. Design specifications or prototype schematic.

query: Prototype? Elaborate.
response: A development stage prototype schematic at Utopia Planitia. Drafting room five of the Mars Station, Stardate 40174.

query: Perfect. Recreate it in holodeck three. Stay with me, Doc.
Ready, computer?
Access denied. Personal logs are restricted.
Computer, did I ask for a simulation?
response: Affirmative. You asked Doctor Brahms to show you which system could accept reactants at a faster rate. By accessing available imagery, an adequate facsimile was possible.

query: Computer, generate a cross section image of the dilithium crystal chamber. What about re-orienting the crystal?
Adjustments to dilithium crystal chamber complete.
Impact analysis, computer.
response: Warp energy has increased fourteen percent. Reactants per unit time remaining steady.

query: Computer, do you have any, you know, personality on file for Doctor Brahms?
Did she ever debate at the intergalactic caucuses on Chaya Seven?
response: Doctor Brahms attended Chaya Seven caucuses on the following stardates

query: Computer, if you add data from all these sources, could you synthesise a true representation of Doctor Brahms?
response: There would be a nine point three seven percent margin of error in the interactive responses from the facsimile.

query: End simulation. Fatal exposure.
Computer, reduce thrust levels another four percent. Adjust trajectory angle to compensate. Begin simulation again.
End simulation. Fatal exposure.
Deflector shield failure. Lethal radiation levels. Fatal exposure in twenty six minutes.
Computer, override standard procedure.
Computer!
Computer, reinstate holodeck three programme.
Computer, resume holodeck three programme.
Computer, exit holodeck. End programme.
